steps.title: Track Max's desire
steps.content: The passage centers on Max wanting to visit La Reina. He has begged Papa to take him to see the ruins, imagines becoming a hero among his friends by crossing the haunted gates, and hopes this summer will finally be the time Papa agrees.

steps.title: Keep the purpose centered
steps.content: Those details all build Max's eagerness to see La Reina, so the overall purpose is about that desire. B invents pride from Max's father, C contradicts Max's hopeful view of summer, and D introduces a fear of growing up that the passage never mentions. The answer is A.
